behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are always identical, and keys from different parameters never collide.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is key-generation logic itself (determinism/canonicalization/hash), with no required module handoff or I/O in the behavior text. Mock-miss check: this can fail inside one function/object. Rejected Integration/System because behavior text does not require cross-module propagation, DB, network, subprocess, or OS primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Unit/property-style test for the key generator: same parameter sets (including reordered map inputs) produce identical keys; distinct parameter sets across a broad corpus do not collide; include edge normalization cases (nulls, casing, whitespace, numeric/string forms) handled by the key function.

behaviors.description: The TTL for a cache entry is computed correctly based on the data category's configured freshness rule (e.g., user profiles = 5 minutes, product catalog = 1 hour).
behaviors.justification: Behavior targets TTL computation from category->freshness mapping; minimum real failure is wrong mapping/arithmetic in one local boundary. No explicit infra boundary is stated. Rejected Integration because the behavior does not specify config loading/wiring failure, only computed TTL correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Unit test for TTL calculator with injected in-memory rules and fixed clock: verify each category returns exact TTL, unknown category fallback behavior, and boundary values at rule edges.

behaviors.description: When memory usage exceeds the configured threshold, the eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU as specified in the configuration.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff: memory-usage signal + configuration + strategy selector must interact correctly. Unit tests of a selector function may miss wiring/state propagation bugs between these modules. Integration-brake: no explicit need for real OS memory metrics/network/subprocess in behavior text; no AI/ML dependency; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System because text says "configured threshold" and does not require real OS memory instrumentation.
behaviors.test_description: Integration test wiring memory monitor, config provider, and eviction manager in-process: drive usage below/above threshold and assert active policy transitions LRU->LFU (and optional hysteresis behavior if defined) using real module interfaces.

behaviors.description: A write to the underlying PostgreSQL table invalidates all cache entries whose keys match the affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly anchors to real PostgreSQL table writes causing cache invalidation. Real failure boundary can depend on DB commit semantics, trigger/notification/change-capture behavior, and timing/order with invalidation consumer. Mocked DB can hide these. Rejected Integration because behavior text specifically requires invalidation driven by an actual PostgreSQL write boundary.
behaviors.test_description: System test with real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer: seed rows and corresponding cache entries, execute INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE on target table, wait for invalidation path, then assert all matching keys are removed and non-matching keys remain.

behaviors.description: The eviction policy removes the correct entries — least-recently-used under LRU, least-frequently-used under LFU — when the cache is full.
behaviors.justification: This is policy algorithm correctness for local cache state (recency/frequency bookkeeping and victim selection). No explicit external boundary required by behavior text. Rejected Integration/System because the failure can be reproduced within one cache object without real DB/OS/network/subprocess.
behaviors.test_description: Unit tests on cache policy implementation: construct full cache, perform deterministic access patterns, then insert new key and assert exact victim under LRU and LFU; include tie-breaking cases defined by spec.
